title: 2 Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
body_text:
2	Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
2.1	Definitions
Branch Capture
Branch Capture, a form of remote deposit, is the process financial institutions use to scan images of cheques, money orders, MICR-encoded Canadian Savings Bonds, drafts, or Receiver General Warrants received on deposit using scanners and capture software in their back office. Financial institution staff transmit images of these items to Central 1 for processing.
Branch Number
The branch number is a 5-digit number used to identify the branch of a Canadian financial institution. The branch number and the institution number make up the transit number.
Chargeback
A chargeback is an item deposited by your financial institution that is subsequently dishonoured by the drawee branch.
Clearing
Clearing is the process of exchanging and reconciling payment items that result in a transfer of funds from one financial institution to another.
Clearing Replacement Document (CRD)
Clearing Replacement Documents (CRDs) are official documents that meet the specifications of CPA Standard 014, Clearing Replacement Document Design Standard. CRDs can be used in place of an original paper payment item for presentment and return purposes in accordance with CPA Rule A10, Image Rule.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre Users
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications. Client Centre Users canât manage User Management Security Officers or other Client Centre Users.
Corporate Capture
Corporate Capture, a form of remote deposit, is the process financial institution business customers use to scan images of cheques, money orders, drafts, or Receiver General Warrants received on deposit using scanners and capture software from the business customerâs location. Business customers transmit images of these items to Central 1 for processing.
CPA
CPA means Canadian Payments Association, also known as Payments Canada.
CPS Admin
The Central Portal System (CPS) Admin application is used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users and User Management Security Officers.
CRCD
Cheque Redemption Control Directorate (CRCD) is a branch of Public Works and Government Services Canada. CRCD is responsible for verifying the authenticity of Receiver General Warrants.
Data Centre
A data centre is the organizational unit designated by a drawee to which its items are directed at a regional exchange point.
Drawee Branch
A drawee branch is the financial institution to which a debit payment item is addressed. The drawee branch is directed to pay the specified amount of money.
Drawer
A drawer is a person who signs the cheque ordering the drawee branch to pay the specified amount of money stated on it.
Endorsement
An endorsement is the signature made by the holder of an item on the back of the item when negotiating (transferring) it.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download files from Central 1 and upload files to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
Identification Stamp
An identification stamp is a stamp affixed to the back of an item by a financial institution that is involved in the exchange of that item.
Image File
An image file is a file that Central 1 provides to financial institutions or their service providers that contains images of cleared cheques drawn on customersâ accounts. These cheque images can be included in customersâ statements.
Incoming Clearing Item
An incoming clearing item is a cheque, draft, or money order that is drawn on your financial institution or a customer of your financial institution, deposited by the payee, and sent through the clearing for debit to the drawerâs account.
Incoming Wire
An incoming wire is a funds transfer created by another financial institution that credits your financial institution, an account holder of your financial institution, or a non-account holder.
Inter-Institution Deposit
An inter-institution deposit is a deposit of items that are returned to another financial institution if dishonoured. The following are examples of items that are inter-institution:
Non-member ATM deposits
Inter-Credit Union (ICU) deposits
Cheques included in ICBC agent deposits (BC credit unions only)
Institution Number
The institution number is the 3-digit number assigned by the CPA that identifies the financial institution (e.g., 809 is for BC credit unions and 828 for Ontario credit unions). The branch number and the institution number make up the transit number. The institution number is sometimes known as a route number.
MICR
Magnetic Ink Character Recognition (MICR) is the machine recognition of characters printed with ink that contains particles of a magnetic material.
Mobile Deposits
Mobile deposits are cheques, money orders, drafts, or Receiver General Warrants that are deposited by a customer to their account at your financial institution, using an application on a mobile device. Central 1âs Deposit Anywhereâ¢ is a mobile deposit application.
OFAC
The Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) administers and enforces economic and trade sanctions based on US foreign policy and national security goals in the US.
Official Image
An official image is an image of an eligible bill created by or on behalf of a financial institution in accordance with bylaws, rules, or standards made under the
Canadian Payments Act
, together with any data in relation to the eligible bill prepared per those bylaws, rules, and standards. It includes a display, a printout, a copy, or any other output of that image and that data created by or on behalf of a financial institution in accordance with those bylaws, rules, and standards. Each cheque that is acceptable for exchange in accordance with CPA Rule A1 or K1 is an eligible bill, as defined in the
Bills of Exchange Act
.
On-Us Item
An on-us item is a cheque that is deposited to an account held at a financial institution and is drawn on an account held at the same financial institution.
ORS
Online Return System (ORS) is a web-based system that facilitates the return of cheques. It also allows users to research deposit items and incoming clearing items and to reinstate cheques. ORS also facilitates the return of AFT transactions and the creation of AFT Notice of Change transactions.
Piggyback
A piggyback is a scanner misfeed where multiple items, usually two, are captured together in one image on the Branch Capture and Corporate Capture applications. Piggybacks contain the front image of one item with either the front or back of another behind it. When piggybacks occur, the deposit is out of balance. Users must locate the physical items that created the piggyback, delete the piggyback image from the deposit, and rescan each item separately within the deposit.
Qualified Item
Qualified items are cheques or drafts that meet CPA standards and are MICR-encoded with the transit number and dollar value. See CPA, Standard 006, Specifications for MICR-Encoded Payment Items.
Receiver General Warrant
A Receiver General Warrant is an authorization for the payment of money drawn on or by the Government of Canada and payable by the Government of Canada. Tax refunds are a common example of these warrants.
Remote Deposit Capture
Remote Deposit Capture (RDC) is the process of scanning cheques, money orders, drafts, or Receiver General Warrants. Images of the items are transmitted to Central 1 for processing. The physical cheque can be imaged on a mobile device, at a branch, at a corporate office, or at an ATM.
ServiceNow
ServiceNow is the web-based ticketing application used to report service issues, request changes to existing services, or submit questions to Central 1.
Transit Number
A transit number is the unique code used to identify financial institutions in Canada. The transit number consists of a leading zero, the 3-digit institution number, and the 5-digit branch number (e.g., 080912010, 082812010).
Unlisted Transit Number
An unlisted transit number is a 9-digit number in the format of a standard transit number (i.e., 080912345) that Central 1 assigns financial institution business customers and financial institution branches that donât have their own transit number.
In some cases, the financial institution assigns the unlisted transit number to its business customer and/or branch. Business customers need an unlisted transit number for Corporate Capture, while branches that donât have a transit number need an unlisted transit number for Branch Capture.
Unlisted transit numbers arenât issued by or registered with Payments Canada and dishonoured items will never be returned to these numbers.
Unqualified Item
Unqualified items are cheques or drafts that donât meet CPA standards for a qualified item or canât be imaged by Central 1âs imaging equipment. Central 1 determines whether an item is qualified or unqualified.
User Management
User Management is an application used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. Itâs also used to edit and delete other User Management Security Officers and to manage 2-step security tokens. Eventually, User Management will replace CPS Admin.
User Management Security Officer
User Management Security Officers use the CPS Admin or User Management application to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. They can also delete and modify other User Management Security Officers (with some exceptions) via the User Management application. User Management Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications (e.g., Branch Capture).
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
Items Acceptable for Deposit
Question 1
Are there restrictions on what types of items we can accept for deposit?
Answer
Yes. The item type, currency, and country of the drawee branch affect whether Central 1 can accept an item on deposit. See Section 5.2, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Branch Capture, and Section 5.3, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Corporate Capture.
Question 2
Is our financial institution required to cash Receiver General Warrants for non-members?
Answer
Yes, but only up to $1,750. The Government of Canada indemnifies your financial institution up to $1,750 if your financial institution follows specific identification and endorsement procedures. See Section 5.5, Receiver General Warrants.
Preparing Deposits for Paper Exchange
Question 3
Do we have to use a tape listing when preparing a cheque bundle? Or can we use a spreadsheet instead?
Answer
Some financial institutions have an internal process that automatically generates a list of deposit items in a spreadsheet format. A spreadsheet (or alternate document) can be used in lieu of a tape listing. The cheques must be kept in the same order as they appear on the spreadsheet.
Foreign Funds Deposits
Question 4
Can we send foreign funds cheques on deposit to Central 1?
Answer
Central 1 only accepts Canadian and US dollar cheques on deposit.
If Central 1 canât accept a deposit item (e.g., Australian Dollar cheques), you can:
Send the item to the drawee branch on collection.
Have your customer request that the sender provide an incoming wire instead.
Process the item via FX Notes Plus, if the currency is accepted (see the FX Notes Plus Part for more information).
Collection Items
Question 5
What does it mean to âsend a cheque on collectionâ?
Answer
Sending a cheque on collection means that your financial institution seeks settlement directly from the drawee branch. Settlement is sent to your financial institution. The settlement period may be lengthy, and the drawee branch may deduct substantial charges.
Question 6
When do we have to send a cheque on collection?
Answer
Send a cheque on collection if a customer presents a cheque that Central 1 canât accept on deposit. Collection requests must be sent directly to the drawee branch. See Section 5.2, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Branch Capture, and Section 5.3, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Corporate Capture, because some cheques canât be accepted even on collection.
Question 7
Do we have to accept cheques from customers where settlement through the collections process is required?
Answer
No. Your financial institution can establish a policy on whether it accepts cheques on collection. Your financial institution can also establish minimum amounts. Central 1 recommends a $300 (Canadian equivalent) minimum because pass-through charges can be substantial.
Deposited Items
Question 8
Can we research mobile deposits, chargebacks, and items deposited via Branch and Corporate Capture?
Answer
Yes. Authorized users can view images of mobile deposits on the Online Return System (ORS). Central 1 creates an electronic deposit slip for every 200 mobile deposits and this deposit slip is also available on ORS.
Chargebacks can be searched, viewed, and printed on ORS.
ORS includes transaction codes for Corporate Capture. For example, you can search for deposits using the transaction code 9300 for CAD items or 9350 for USD items and list all corporate deposits for a given day or range of days.
See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for more information.
Mobile Deposits (Deposit Anywhere)
Question 9
What are mobile deposits?
Answer
Mobile deposits are cheques, money orders, drafts, and Receiver General Warrants that are deposited by a customer to their account at your financial institution using an application on a mobile device.
Question 10
Are mobile deposits included in any Central 1 reports?
Answer
Yes. The RDLC â Remote Deposit System, Mobile Remote Deposits report lists individual mobile deposits.
The ADC â Acknowledgement of Canadian Dollar Deposits report also includes the deposit slip totals for mobile deposits. Central 1 automatically generates a deposit slip for every 200 mobile deposits.
Question 11
Will the deposit show immediately on the customerâs account?
Answer
Yes, if you have implemented Central 1âs Real-Time messaging and your banking host has made the necessary changes to support Deposit Anywhere. Your customer will see the funds in their account as soon as your host updates the account and makes the funds available for viewing. This service isnât mandatory, and if your financial institution doesnât implement real-time messaging, Deposit Anywhereâ¢ will still work correctly, but the deposit wonât appear on the customerâs account until after normal overnight processing of the item.
Question 12
What if the customer takes a bad picture of the cheque?
Answer
Deposit Anywhere performs over 30 Image Quality Assurance (IQA) tests on each picture submitted to ensure that the image is accurate and that each field can be read. If the image doesnât meet all the criteria, it fails, and the customer is asked to take a new picture.
Question 13
Is there a fraud risk with Deposit Anywhere?
Answer
There is a fraud risk with any service that transfers funds, and Deposit Anywhere is no exception. However, there are no additional fraud risks that donât already exist. Itâs the responsibility of your financial institution to determine how to manage the risk of the service.
Question 14
What happens if the same cheque is deposited twice?
Answer
Deposit Anywhere will recognize duplicate items that are deposited through any implementation of Deposit Anywhere. However, if an item is deposited via Deposit Anywhere and then via an ATM, the software wonât detect the duplication. The duplicate will have to be returned using the current CPA rules for dishonouring duplicate items (âDuplicate Paymentâ with a 90-day return time frame) by the payor financial institution.
Question 15
Can a customer deposit USD items?
Answer
No. Payment items must be payable in Canadian dollars and drawn on a financial institution domiciled in Canada, or in the case of Receiver General Warrants, drawn on the Government of Canada.
Question 16
Are limits set per customer or per a customerâs account?
Answer
Limits are set per customer. Financial institutions can set a maximum deposit amount and a maximum number of deposits per day or week. Financial institutions can also establish policies on determining which customers use mobile deposits. As with other products, âknow your customerâ before enabling mobile deposit functionality.
Question 17
What are the rules around destroying cheques deposited via Deposit Anywhere?
Answer
CPA Rule A10, Image Rule, states that payment items deposited by image exchange must be destroyed within 120 days. CPA doesnât stipulate a minimum retention period. However, items with poor image quality can be dishonoured up to 90 days after clearing. Your financial institution must inform customers of the time frame for retaining physical cheques and provide guidance on how the cheques should be destroyed.
Branch Capture Deposits
Question 18
How do we process inter-institution deposits?
Answer
When processing inter-institution deposits in Branch Capture, the Capture User must manually enter the itemâs branch number in the Depositor Transit field, and the institution number in the Depositor Route field. This information is found on the back of the item. See the physical item or the itemâs image in Branch Capture.
Question 19
How are unqualified items processed?
Answer
Central 1 will create a PDF file that contains images of unqualified items including chargebacks and bulky items from Calgary and Winnipeg regions and US southbound chargebacks.
The PDF files are available from the File and Report Exchange service for financial institutions. The File and Report Exchange can be accessed via the Client Centre or via direct connection (SFTP).
Question 20
How are CRA payments processed?
Answer
CRA business remittances that can be remitted electronically are processed on Central 1âs Business Taxes. This is a stand-alone application that isnât connected to a banking host for ease of implementation.
Business remittances that arenât available electronically must be submitted to the CRA manually.
Question 21
What is the Branch Capture deadline?
Answer
Deposits must be transmitted to Central 1 each business day by 7 pm local time. Deposits transmitted after 7 pm local time are processed the next business day.
Question 22
How many times a day should we transmit deposits to Central 1?
Answer
You must transmit deposits at least three times a day (e.g., mid-morning, mid-afternoon, and end of day). Donât transmit all your deposits to Central 1 at the end of day.
Question 23
Can we include photocopies of cheques in my deposit?
Answer
No, you canât include photocopies of cheques in your deposit.
Corporate Capture Deposits
Question 24
Do we have to use the same user type combinations and workflows for all business customers?
Answer
No. Your financial institution can permit some business customers to perform all deposit processing tasks and other business customers to capture and submit deposits for review and transmission by your financial institution staff. Deposit permissions are based on the user type(s) assigned to users.
Question 25
We have decided to have staff monitor, review, and transmit Corporate Capture deposits. How many business customer locations can be assigned to an individual Corporate Supervisor user at our financial institution?
Answer
A maximum of 44 individual business customer locations (9-digit unlisted transits separated by a comma) can be assigned to a single Client Centre user ID.
Question 26
Can our financial institution users have both Corporate Capture and Branch Capture user types assigned to a single Client Centre user ID and participate in both types of deposits?
Answer
No. Corporate Capture and Branch Capture share a common platform; however, Corporate Capture supports real-time deposits to customer accounts and has a different workflow. You must assign the Corporate Capture application to staff who arenât assigned to the Branch Capture application.
Question 27
Can a single user at our financial institution be assigned the Capture and Supervisor user types if we permit the business customer to perform all deposit tasks? Or does there need to be a separate user for each role?
Answer
A single user can have both the Capture and Supervisor user types. Combining the roles into a single individualâs user ID streamlines the user experience by removing some additional steps but relies on the individual to ensure the deposit is reviewed carefully before completing. When this type of user selects âCompleteâ on a balanced and error-free deposit, the deposit is automatically transmitted for processing. Transmitted deposits canât be edited, deleted, or recalled.
Question 28
Will an alert be generated to notify branch staff when a business customerâs Corporate Capture deposits are ready for review/transmission by a financial institution Supervisor User?
Answer
No. The application doesnât generate alerts. Your Supervisor Users must monitor their Corporate Capture deposit list queues regularly. Deposits shouldnât be left until the end of day for review and transmission.
Question 29
Can the Corporate Capture software detect cheques that arenât acceptable for deposit (e.g., items that are post- or stale-dated)?
Answer
No. The software only reads the MICR line and amount of the item. The business customer is required to verify that cheques are acceptable for deposit before scanning the items.
Question 30
Can limits be set for the business customer or individual users at an organization?
Answer
Deposit limits to the corporate organization can be controlled in your banking system if built in as part of your real-time integration. Limits canât be set for individual users at the organization.
Question 31
Can the business customer create a deposit in Corporate Capture and then add to it all day before completing it?
Answer
Deposits shouldnât be created until the business user is prepared to complete them. This ensures the deposits are processed in a timely manner and avoids the potential for scanning duplicate items in error.
Question 32
How many cheques can be scanned for each deposit?
Answer
For businesses with significant cheque volume, we recommend each deposit contain no more than 100 items to help facilitate error correction and balancing of the deposit.
Question 33
Will each cheque in a deposit appear as a separate item on the business customerâs account?
Answer
No. The total of the items included in a deposit will appear as an entry in the customerâs account.
Question 34
Is the business customer required to have a US dollar account to deposit US cheques?
Answer
Yes. The US account information can be provided to Central 1 when the business customer is set up for Corporate Capture, or later if a US account is added. US dollar cheques drawn on financial institutions domiciled in Canada or the United States can be deposited through Corporate Capture. The business customer must ensure the appropriate US dollar account is selected when scanning US dollar items (i.e., US dollar items must be sorted and scanned separately from Canadian dollar items).
Question 35
Can a deposit that is in-process be modified or deleted using a computer that isnât attached to the scanner?
Answer
No. Deposits in-process must be deleted or modified using the scanner computer, as the application sends commands to the scanner driver.
Question 36
Can an in-process deposit be modified or deleted by a user other than the user who created the deposit?
Answer
Yes, but only if the deposit hasnât been transmitted. The deposit must first be assigned to another capture user by the Corporate Supervisor to delete, correct, or complete.
Question 37
Can deposits be disapproved or transmitted from a computer that isnât attached to a scanner?
Answer
Yes. Corporate Supervisor Users can perform the disapproval or transmit functions from any computer, as these processes donât involve the scanner driver.
Question 38
If the detected errors are corrected and the deposit is balanced, does this mean the deposit is error-free?
Answer
No. While the Corporate Capture application performs duplicate detection and some image quality tests, itâs possible that not all errors have been detected or that detected errors have been correctly resolved by the customer. In addition, the application doesnât validate if the item was deposited in the correct currency, or if the item is post/stale-dated, signed, or endorsed.
Contingency Procedures
Question 40
How do we proceed if thereâs an equipment or business continuity failure at our credit union and we canât scan the items?
Answer
Equipment Failure
â
Central 1 recommends that each branch of your financial institution maintain a backup scanner on site.
Network Failure
â
if your network is down for a short period, items can be held until the network problem has been resolved. If the problem is likely to be unresolved until after the daily cut off, you could send the items to another branch for processing or hold the items until the next day and carry the float.
END
